scenario: Forge Manufacturer models a Camunda 8.8 quality inspection workflow that handles 7000 instances per day. The architect reviews which BPMN activity marker icons the coverage page lists with an implementation marker and which appear without one before locking the modeling palette.
question: Which activity marker icon in the BPMN coverage page is shown without an implementation marker in Camunda 8.8?

options.text: The parallel multi-instance marker icon on the BPMN coverage markers section appears without an implementation marker for execution in Camunda 8.8
The loop marker icon on the BPMN coverage markers section appears without an implementation marker for execution in Camunda 8.8
The compensation marker icon on the BPMN coverage markers section appears without an implementation marker for execution in Camunda 8.8
The ad-hoc marker icon on the BPMN coverage markers section appears without an implementation marker for execution in Camunda 8.8

a.text: Incorrect. The BPMN coverage page lists the parallel multi-instance marker icon with the implementation marker, indicating Camunda 8 executes parallel multi-instance activities.

b.text: Correct. The BPMN coverage page lists the loop marker icon without an implementation marker, indicating Camunda 8 does not execute the standard BPMN loop marker on activities.

c.text: Incorrect. The BPMN coverage page lists the compensation marker icon with the implementation marker, indicating Camunda 8 executes compensation handlers for activities.

d.text: Incorrect. The BPMN coverage page lists the ad-hoc marker icon with the implementation marker, indicating Camunda 8 executes ad-hoc subprocesses with the tilde marker.

explanation: In the BPMN coverage markers section, parallel multi-instance, sequential multi-instance, compensation, and ad-hoc markers carry the implementation marker, while the loop marker icon appears without one.
